other_observers: none; I may have been the original finder of this individual bird. However, I was aware that the Higbee Cemetery was one of the best places in the state to find Vermillion Flycatchers.
description: I observed the bird perched on a phone line, next to a Cassin's Kingbird.  It was much shorter than the kingbird (maybe 60% as long), with a much more compact structure.  The upperparts were medium brown, with no markings.  The face was pale brown, with a large, bold, dark brown eye. The bill was thin and dark.  The throat was whitish, unstreaked.  The breast was light gray, with bold, coarse streaking running down to the belly.  The belly and undertail coverts, except for the central (median) third of the belly, were a dull pinkish (maybe rosy with a tinge of orange). The median part of the belly was off-white.  The sides of the tail had narrow strips of off-white color.  The upper surface of the tail was darker than the back.Behaviors: It was perched on a phone line, with a posture typical for a flycatcher.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
